page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.4xlarge is a burstable instance type powered by the Intel Xeon 8163 processor, operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 virtual CPUs mapping to 16 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The memory configuration is constrained to 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low resource density of 1.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 10% to 25% tier, reflecting the burstable nature and CPU credit limitations of the ecs.t5 family. However, multi-core benchmarks, including Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core tests, reach average mid-tier performance. Memory bandwidth is moderate, though the Passmark memory score is weak. The instance handles basic database operations and static web serving at average throughput levels, but struggles with resource-intensive tasks like medium-sized LLM inference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.t5-c1m1.4xlarge offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads requiring high parallel vCPU availability without high memory demands. Because CPU allocation is burstable, it is highly economical for non-continuous, spike-prone workloads. It is best suited for lightweight multi-threaded applications, development and testing environments, basic web serving, and low-traffic database operations that do not require sustained peak CPU performance or large memory footprints.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.4xlarge is a burstable x86_64 server configured with 16 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz with a 33 MB L3 cache. This instance features a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, lacks local storage, and contains no GPU accelerators. Operating under a KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core performance but average multi-core capabilities, alongside moderate memory bandwidth and average database operation speeds. Due to its burstable CPU allocation model, this server offers a highly cost-efficient profile for workloads with intermittent or spike-prone CPU utilization. It is best suited for budget-conscious, multi-threaded applications, software compilation, development environments, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.4xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers cost-effective, multi-core performance for spike-prone, low-memory workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.4xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: This burstable virtual machine delivers multi core processing capabilities for budget conscious workloads that experience intermittent or spike prone resource demands.
bullet_points: 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs with a burstable CPU allocation model
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average multi-core benchmark performance with weak single-core metrics
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and KVM hypervisor integration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for development environments, light web serving, and bursty workloads
